name: serverkit-k8s
description: Manage remote Kubernetes clusters from the panel. Add a cluster by pasting its kubeconfig (stored encrypted, one row per cluster) and the extension shells out to kubectl to show cluster health, nodes, namespaces, workloads, pods with live logs, services, and ingresses, plus scale, restart, delete, and apply-manifest actions. Most panels never touch Kubernetes, so it ships as an opt-in extension and needs no cluster on the panel host itself.
author: ServerKit

command_palette.label: Kubernetes
command_palette.keywords: k8s kubernetes kubectl cluster node namespace pod deployment workload statefulset daemonset service ingress scale restart apply kubeconfig
